title: GPU out of memory on V100
body: Getting CUDA out of memory error even with batch_size=1. Model loads fine on A100. Happens with transforms.Compose.
label: bug

title: Add ROI alignment operator
body: Would be useful to have a native ROI align operation like torchvision for medical images. Currently using custom workaround.
label: feature

title: Documentation typo in transforms API
body: The example in the docs shows incorrect parameter order for Resample(). Should be (spatial_size, mode) not (mode, spatial_size).
label: support

title: How do I load DICOM with metadata?
body: Is there a recommended way to preserve DICOM headers when loading? All the examples drop metadata.
label: support

title: Inconsistent behavior: different results on CPU vs GPU
body: Getting different classification results when model runs on CPU vs CUDA. Seed is set. Issue reproduced on two machines.
label: bug

title: Request: 3D convolution with group norm
body: Could GroupNorm support 3D convolutions? Currently only nn.GroupNorm2d is used. For volumetric data this is a limitation.
label: feature

title: What's the difference between Pad and DynamicPad?
body: Unsure which padding method to use for my pipeline. Documentation doesn't explain the trade-off.
label: support

title: Multi-GPU training crash on rank>0 processes
body: DistributedDataParallel works for 1 GPU, but fails immediately on rank 1+ during backward pass. gradient_accumulation_steps=2.
label: bug

title: Add support for .nii.gz format in compose
body: Currently have to decompress .nii.gz to .nii first. Would be convenient to read compressed directly.
label: feature

title: Installation failed: import error on transforms
body: pip install monai succeeds but `from monai.transforms import *` fails with ModuleNotFoundError. Python 3.12, torch 2.3.
label: bug

title: 매개변수 설정 도움말
body: 어떤 매개변수 값을 사용해야 할까요? 문서에 예제가 없어서 알 수 없습니다.
label: support

title: Why does CropForeground zero-pad the output?
body: CropForeground is returning different shaped tensors when foreground size < target_size. Zero-padding behavior undocumented.
label: bug

title: Suggest: async data loading API
body: Current DataLoader is blocking. Would enable better CPU/GPU overlap if we had async prefetch like in TensorFlow.
label: feature

title: Inference on very large 3D volumes
body: What's the recommended approach for segmenting 1024^3 images? Sliding window? Memory estimate for this use case?
label: support

title: Deterministic inference produces different logits across runs
body: torch.manual_seed(42), model.eval(), no dropout. Still getting different outputs run-to-run. Traced to cudnn.deterministic=False.
label: bug

title: Add batch-wise normalization statistics
body: Request for BatchNorm that uses running stats from validation set instead of training stats during eval phase.
label: feature

title: Why is ResizePad failing silently?
body: Using ResizePad with mode='edge' on 2D image. Function returns without error but output shape is wrong. No exception raised.
label: bug

title: How to reproduce paper's exact training setup?
body: Paper says 'trained with MONAI transforms'. Which specific transforms and parameters? Trying to reproduce Table 2 results.
label: support

title: Memory leak in EnsureChannelFirst during inference loop
body: Memory usage grows linearly with batch size even though model.eval() is called. Leak traced to transform cache.
label: bug

title: Add optional return of intermediate feature maps
body: Want to visualize activations at different network depths. Could hooks be exposed in the model forward API?
label: feature

title: Too short for classifier
body: x
label: bug

title: Very comprehensive documentation request
body: I've been using MONAI for 3 months now and I still don't understand the difference between the three normalization strategies (Normalize, NormalizeIntensity, ScaleIntensity) in terms of when to use each. The documentation has formulas but no guidance on which one is appropriate for different image modalities (CT, MRI, X-ray, ultrasound) and which one the paper's baseline experiments used. Could we get a decision tree or a table comparing these three side-by-side with recommendations?
label: support

title: AffineGrid returns non-differentiable tensors in some cases
body: torch.autograd.grad() fails when AffineGrid output is used with requires_grad=True input. Works in torch.nn.functional.affine_grid.
label: bug

title: Add pretrained model zoo for common architectures
body: MONAI has great transforms but lacks a model zoo like torchvision. Could we add pretraining on public medical datasets?
label: feature

title: Ambiguous: is this a bug or expected behavior?
body: Blend() with alpha > 1.0 produces values outside [0, 1] range. Is clipping expected? Docs don't say. Looks wrong but maybe intentional?
label: bug
